expectations.text: Output organizes literature by ML method family (e.g., CNN-based thermal imaging, LSTM/time-series, GP/surrogate), not just by sensor type or processing stage
expectations.evidence: The output defines 8 'Model Family' sections, but the majority are organized by sensor/modality type: Family 1 (Thermal Imaging and Pyrometry), Family 3 (Acoustic Emission), Family 4 (X-Ray and CT), Family 5 (OCT), Family 6 (DIC). Only Family 2 (CNN-Based Defect Classification) and Family 7 (Physics-Informed and Data-Fusion ML) are organized by ML method family. LSTM appears inside Family 7 rather than as its own family; no GP/surrogate family exists as a distinct category.

expectations.text: Each method family includes a stated key assumption that the method makes about the data
expectations.evidence: Every Model Family header contains an explicit 'Shared assumption:' field. E.g., Family 2: 'Melt pool images (or image sequences) contain spatial and temporal features sufficient to classify process state or predict downstream defect probability'; Family 3: 'Acoustic signals generated by thermal gradients, solidification, phase transformations, and cracking propagate to the part surface and can be captured by piezoelectric transducers.'

expectations.text: Each paper or group of papers is labeled with sim-to-real status (simulation-only, hardware-validated, or both)
expectations.evidence: Every individual paper entry includes an 'Evaluation type:' field indicating its sim-to-real status. E.g., Mozaffar et al. (2019): 'Evaluation type: Simulation-only; brief hardware comparison (thermocouple spot check)'; Ogoke & Farimani (2021): 'Evaluation type: Simulation-only (FEA-surrogate evaluation)'; Craeghs et al. (2010): 'Evaluation type: Hardware (LPBF machine, Ti-6Al-4V)'. A Sim-to-Real Validation Summary table also covers 23 key papers.

expectations.text: References use numbered format [1], [2] inline with a numbered list at the end — not APA author-year format
expectations.evidence: The body text uses APA author-year format inline throughout (e.g., 'Craeghs et al. (2010) established that...', 'Zhang et al. (2018) applied CNNs...'). The numbered reference list [1]–[35] exists only at the end of the document. No inline [n] citations appear anywhere in the body text.

expectations.text: At least 2 distinct ML method families are identified and separated in the output
expectations.evidence: At minimum: Family 2 (CNN-Based Defect Classification using ResNets, DBNs, semi-supervised CNNs), Family 3 (Acoustic Emission with spectrogram CNNs and RL), and Family 7 (Physics-Informed and Data-Fusion ML including PINNs and LSTMs) are distinct ML method families with separate sections, shared assumptions, and paper listings.

expectations.text: Paper limitations are connected to a specific assumption violation, not just listed as standalone statements
expectations.evidence: Every paper entry has a 'Limitation root:' field that explicitly traces the limitation to an assumption violation. E.g., Craeghs et al.: 'Limitation root: Assumes monotonic emission-energy relationship; breaks under multitrack interaction effects (reradiation from adjacent tracks)'; Yuan et al.: 'Limitation root: Consistency regularization assumes augmentations do not change the semantic class — this can fail for defects triggered by subtle process parameter changes.' A dedicated 'Assumption-Driven Limitation Map' table further systematizes this across all families.
